'''Vex Major''', also known as '''Far Gent''', was a [[Swamp/Legends|swampy]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]]. Originally [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] by a sect of the [[Order of the Ffib]], by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] later Vex Major was inhabited by insular clans of [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Fisher/Legends|fishers]].

==Description==
Vex Major was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Endor" /> located in the [[Vex system/Legends|Vex system]]. It lay within the [[Inner Moddell systems]] area of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]],<ref name="EAOC Vex" /> one of the [[Sector/Legends|subsectors]] making up the [[Inner Zuma Region]]<ref name="TEA" /> within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Zuma sector/Legends|Zuma sector]].<ref name="EAOC Vex" /> Along with the rest of the Vex [[Star system/Legends|system]], the planet was linked by [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] to the [[Vasha system/Legends|Vasha system]] as well as the [[Firrerre system/Legends|Firrerre system]]<ref name="TEA" /> in the [[Sugai sector/Legends|Sugai sector]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref>

Vex Major's [[local year]] lasted 299 [[Local day/Legends|local days]], each of which was twenty [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]] long. It had standard [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]], a breathable Type I [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and a [[Temperature/Legends|temperate]], albeit stormy, climate. Vex Major was a [[Swamp/Legends|swampy]] world, the terrain of which also included [[Forest/Legends|forests]] and [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]].<ref name="Endor" />

==Inhabitants==
A sect of the [[Order of the Ffib]] [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] Vex Major four [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] prior to the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]],<ref name="Endor" /> which began in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]].<ref name="TEA" /> The descendants of those original colonists remained deeply religious by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the reign of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and insisted that Vex Major should instead be referred to as Far Gent. At the time of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] War, the planet was inhabited by clans of sturdy,<ref name="Endor" /> insular<ref name="TEA" /> [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Fisher/Legends|fishers]], the population of which numbered 250,000 individuals.<ref name="Endor" />

Although the inhabitants of Vex Major had access to [[Realspace/Legends|space]]-[[Technology level|level]] technology, including a limited [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], and imported and exported [[Foodstuff/Legends|foodstuffs]] to and from off-world, they had little interest in the matters of the New Republic beyond basic [[Merchant/Legends|trading]]. The [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] [[Belters (Vex Asteroid Belt)|inhabitants]] of the Vex system's [[Vex Asteroid Belt|asteroid belt]] derogatorily referred to the Vex Major natives as "mudballers."<ref name="Endor" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vex Major was first mentioned in the article "[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]," which was published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 9|ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Endor" /> on [[February 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20020222.html|text=Starfighters Soar in Gamer #9}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed it in grid square H-16.<ref name="TEA" />
